approach: Backfill CR, stated as such: three rendering commits already landed on the CR branch (PR #63) before any spec existed for them. mux/ pins worktree-list PROVENANCE (git owns path/branch/linked/prunable) but is silent on how the table RENDERS those facts, which is the gap the commits filled uncontested. Explore reads source + tests rather than collecting seed intent; the impl gate verifies against the already-green suite rather than a fresh build.

Blast radius small and spec-only: additive scenarios in mux.feature plus a prose rule in mux/README.md. No source change is proposed by this CR — the code is the subject, not the target. Compatibility does not fire (package is 0.0.0, nothing shipped). Clearance does not fire: the existing worktree-list provenance scenarios are untouched and still hold, since the markers render git's facts rather than restating them. No Conflict.

Leash auto-spec, derived: self-assert within the spec leash on additive-only work. The generalized rule being specified is that a one-bit fact earns a marker rather than a column, and that a marker is human-surface only — --format json keeps every field and the absolute path. The ~ half is consistency, not novelty: AXI #10 already states $HOME collapsed to ~ for the home view.

why.floor: No floor fires. Edit class verified structurally, not assumed: 50 insertions, ZERO modified or removed lines across mux.feature and mux/README.md — additive-only, so the file-level @frozen self-clears and no re-open was needed. Clearance does not fire (no existing scenario narrowed; the untouched worktree-list provenance scenarios pin WHOSE facts these are, the new band pins HOW they render — orthogonal aspects, confirmed non-conflicting by two independent cold judges). Compatibility does not fire: package is 0.0.0, nothing shipped. No Conflict.
why.blast: small and spec-only: three added scenarios plus one section comment in mux.feature, one Use Cases bullet and one summary-table row in mux/README.md. This CR proposes no source change — the already-shipped code is its subject, not its target.
why.novelty: low for the ~ half (axi/'s #10 already owes $HOME collapsed to ~ on the home view, so this is consistency across surfaces rather than a new idea) and moderate for the generalization actually being pinned: a one-bit fact earns a marker on the column it is about rather than a column of its own, and a marker is human-surface only.
why.confidence: high — two cold spec-judge rounds at depth 1, each re-deriving its own oracle. Round 1 failed the builder lens on three underdetermination defects (Then without its Given subject; marker glyphs unprotected in the never-frozen README; boundary pinned at the --format json flag rather than the surface, which the corpus's own owed TOON default would have hollowed out). Round 2 independently verified all three fixes real and passed builder, then caught a regression the producer had introduced by taking round 1's optional band-placement nit, cleared by an additive section comment. Mechanical checks green throughout (check:features both files parse; check-spec-state and check-suite reported OK by the judge). Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash; lands provisionally in the async review queue: ratify or kick back.

why.floor: none at the impl gate. This is a backfill: the implementation landed before the spec existed, which is stated plainly in the plan brief and the run-start leash block rather than presented as a build-to-contract. The frozen scenarios were nonetheless written to the OBSERVABLE behavior and then checked against it, not fitted to the code's internals — no scenario names a helper, a module, or a call shape.
why.blast: small — no source change in this CR. The behavior under verification is the three rendering commits already on the branch: cli.ts's worktree-list column set and output.ts's tildify helper.
why.novelty: low — verification only, over an already-green suite.
why.confidence: high — every frozen scenario has a real verification in the shipped suite, checked one by one rather than in aggregate. Scenario 1 (marker not column): the cli.test.ts case asserting the primary renders marked, a linked worktree does not, and no LINKED header remains, plus the case asserting the vanished checkout's path is marked. Scenario 2 (~ shortening, boundary-matched): the cli.test.ts home-rooted case plus output.test.ts's five-case boundary set, including the sibling-prefix near-miss and the root-home edge. Scenario 3 (marker never reaches a structured payload): both JSON cases, which assert the booleans survive, the path is absolute, and the branch field carries no marker text. 179 tests green in the two touched files; pnpm verify 7/7 turbo tasks green on the tree. No dedicated cold impl-judge dispatched for a spec-only CR whose subject is an already-verified implementation; self-asserted within the auto-spec leash.
